bid_claims.quote_text: On April 14, 2014, five parties, comprising four financial buyers and one strategic buyer, submitted preliminary and non-binding indications of interest. The bids received in the preliminary indications of interest ranged from $20.00 per share to $22.00 per share.

On May 9, 2014, Party X submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest to BofA Merrill Lynch, increasing the number of preliminary indications of interest received by us as of such date to six. The bid submitted by Party X ranged from $21.50 to $23.00 per share.

On May 20, 2014, Party Y submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest, bringing the total number of preliminary indications of interest received by us to seven. The indication of interest submitted by Party Y ranged from $19.50 to $20.50 per share.
On February 19, 2015, New Mountain Capital delivered an unsolicited indication of interest to the Company to acquire us for a per share price of $19.25.

On February 26, 2015, New Mountain Capital delivered a revised indication of interest reflecting an increased per share price of $20.05, and indicated that this was the highest price it was willing to offer.
On March 13, 2015, New Mountain Capital communicated that the $20.05 per share offer price was New Mountain Capital's "best and final" offer.

event_claims.description: Board first discussed exploring strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the Company.
event_claims.quote_text: At a June 20, 2013 meeting, our board of directors first discussed the possibility of exploring strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the Company.

event_claims.description: Board decided to engage in a process to consider strategic alternatives and approved BofA Merrill Lynch engagement.
event_claims.quote_text: At a January 28, 2014 board meeting, our board of directors discussed our year to date performance, our anticipated performance against the fiscal year 2014 plan and various factors that could impact our future performance. Following discussions, our board of directors decided to engage in a process to consider strategic alternatives.

event_claims.description: Board approved contacting potential buyers, executing confidentiality agreements, and conducting meetings.
event_claims.quote_text: Our board of directors unanimously approved moving forward with next steps, including contacting the potential buyers on the list discussed by our board of directors, executing confidentiality agreements with such parties and conducting meetings with such parties.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch contacted fifty potential buyers at the direction of the board.
event_claims.quote_text: During the next several weeks, BofA Merrill Lynch, at the direction of our board of directors, contacted fifty potential buyers (comprising twenty-eight strategic buyers and twenty-two financial buyers, including financial buyers who owned businesses similar to our businesses), and we executed confidentiality agreements with a number of the potential buyers.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch distributed a first round process letter with preliminary indications of interest due by April 14, 2014.
event_claims.quote_text: On March 27, 2014, at the direction of our board of directors, BofA Merrill Lynch distributed a first round process letter, which informed potential buyers that non-binding preliminary indications of interest would be due no later than April 14, 2014.

event_claims.description: Five parties submitted preliminary and non-binding indications of interest.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 14, 2014, five parties, comprising four financial buyers and one strategic buyer, submitted preliminary and non-binding indications of interest.

event_claims.description: Party X submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 9, 2014, Party X submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest to BofA Merrill Lynch, increasing the number of preliminary indications of interest received by us as of such date to six.

event_claims.description: Party Y submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 20, 2014, Party Y submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest, bringing the total number of preliminary indications of interest received by us to seven.

event_claims.description: Party X withdrew from pursuing a potential transaction.
event_claims.quote_text: May 14, 2014, prior to receiving access to the electronic data room or meeting with the Company's management, Party X informed representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch that it was no longer interested in pursuing a potential transaction with the Company.

event_claims.description: Board decided to terminate the process to explore potential strategic alternatives.
event_claims.quote_text: At a June 26, 2014 meeting of our board of directors, based on the lack of buyer interest and the uncertainty surrounding the impact of the fire at our aerosol manufacturing facility in Marietta, Georgia, our board of directors decided to terminate the process to explore potential strategic alternatives at that time and to continue to focus on our business and financial performance.

event_claims.description: New Mountain Capital delivered an unsolicited indication of interest at $19.25 per share.
event_claims.quote_text: On February 19, 2015, New Mountain Capital delivered an unsolicited indication of interest to the Company to acquire us for a per share price of $19.25.

event_claims.description: New Mountain Capital delivered a revised indication of interest at $20.05 per share.
event_claims.quote_text: On February 26, 2015, New Mountain Capital delivered a revised indication of interest reflecting an increased per share price of $20.05, and indicated that this was the highest price it was willing to offer.

event_claims.description: Company signed an agreement with New Mountain Capital providing exclusivity through March 31, 2015.
event_claims.quote_text: On February 27, 2015, we signed an agreement with New Mountain Capital extending the term of the confidentiality provision, the "standstill" provision and the employee non-solicitation provision in our original confidentiality agreement with them as well as providing for an exclusivity period with New Mountain Capital through March 31, 2015.

event_claims.description: New Mountain Capital communicated its $20.05 per share offer was best and final.
event_claims.quote_text: On March 13, 2015, New Mountain Capital communicated that the $20.05 per share offer price was New Mountain Capital's "best and final" offer.

event_claims.description: Board authorized extension of exclusivity with New Mountain Capital through April 7, 2015, signed March 18, 2015.
event_claims.quote_text: our board of directors authorized an extension of exclusivity with New Mountain Capital through April 7, 2015, which extension letter was signed on March 18, 2015.

event_claims.description: New Mountain Capital reiterated its $20.05 per share offer was best and final.
event_claims.quote_text: New Mountain Capital reiterated its previous position that the $20.05 per share price offer was New Mountain Capital's "best and final" offer.

event_claims.description: Board unanimously determined the merger agreement and transactions were advisable and in stockholders' and the Company's best interests.
event_claims.quote_text: Following discussion, our board of directors unanimously determined that the merger agreement and the transactions contemplated thereby, including the merger, were advisable and in the best interests of our stockholders and the Company, and our board of directors resolved to recommend that our stockholders approve the adoption of the merger agreement.

event_claims.description: The parties executed the merger agreement and related transaction documents and announced the transaction on April 8, 2015.
event_claims.quote_text: Following the meeting of our board of directors, the parties executed the merger agreement and the related transaction documents and issued a press release announcing the transaction on the morning of April 8, 2015.
